criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose inferior ischemia, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment depression in inferior leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in leads I and aVL
Presence of ST-segment elevation in inferior leads
Presence of inverted T waves in anterior leads
Presence of J-point elevation in inferior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment depression in inferior leads

finding.question: Reagrding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST depression in at least two of the inferior leads, including leads II, III, and aVF?
options: Yes
No

decision.question: based on the finding identified above, does this patient have inferior ischemia?
options: Yes
No
Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose inferior ischemia?
options: Presence of Camel-hump T waves (P on T) in inferior leads
Presence of tall, peaked T waves in inferior leads
Presence of inverted T waves in inferior leads
Presence of flattened T waves in inferior leads
Presence of inverted T waves in anterior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of inverted T waves in inferior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show inverted T waves in at least two of the inferior leads, including leads II, III, and aVF?
options: Yes
No

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have inferior ischemia?
options: Yes
No
Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
